claim: Provider configuration preserves full tool capability under harness boundary and avoids cross-provider model leakage.

evidence.summary: All 10 evidence records from explorer and cross-checker have valid scope field (project), valid source fields (project_code, project_test, project_docs), and include all required fields: path, line, scope, source, summary, why
evidence.why: This confirms evidence structure compliance with the yolo.demand.evidence_result.v1 schema.

evidence.summary: Explorer and cross-checker both independently verified the same code sections for execution_policy, executionConfig provider isolation, and boundary snapshot enforcement
evidence.why: Independent verification of the same code sections by two agents confirms the evidence is reproducible and not dependent on single-agent interpretation.

evidence.summary: Explorer and cross-checker both verified test coverage for provider isolation, tool capability preservation, and boundary enforcement
evidence.why: Test evidence confirms the claims are validated by automated tests, not just code inspection.

evidence.summary: Assumptions are properly separated from facts in both agent results: git ls-files reliability, non-git walkFiles coverage, and provider agent contract compliance are all labeled as assumptions, not presented as evidence
evidence.why: This confirms that unverified claims are not promoted to facts. The assumptions acknowledge boundary conditions that cannot be verified from code/docs alone.

evidence.summary: Both explorer and cross-checker have missing: [] (empty array), confirming no verification gaps were identified
evidence.why: Empty missing array is required when nothing is missing. Both agents correctly followed this protocol requirement.

evidence.summary: Cross-checker independently verified all 10 explorer evidence claims without finding any conflicts or weakening any claim
evidence.why: Cross-check agreement status is pass with zero conflicts, meaning no claims were contradicted or found to be assumptions presented as facts.

evidence.summary: Provider adapter code was independently verified by both agents as having separate branches that never mix configuration namespaces
evidence.why: This is the implementation-level evidence that prevents cross-provider leakage at the argument construction stage.

evidence.summary: Doctrine documentation was independently verified by both agents as confirming the design intent for full tool capability with boundary-based safety
evidence.why: Documentation evidence confirms the implementation matches the documented design intent.

assumptions: The evidence paths and line numbers cited by explorer and cross-checker are accurate reflections of the actual codebase
The test files actually pass when run, not just that the test assertions exist
Git commands in boundary snapshot code succeed in practice, not just in theory
risks: If test suite is not run or does not pass, the test evidence may be misleading despite correct assertions
If boundary snapshot code has runtime errors not visible in static inspection, the enforcement may fail silently
If provider adapter has edge cases not covered by the inspected branches, cross-provider leakage could still occur

result.notes: Verification protocol confirms: (1) All 20 evidence records (10 from explorer, 10 from cross-checker) have proper structure with valid scope, source, path, line, summary, and why fields; (2) Assumptions are properly separated from facts in both agent results and acknowledge boundary conditions; (3) Both agents have missing: [] confirming no identified verification gaps; (4) Cross-checker independently verified all explorer claims with zero conflicts; (5) Evidence spans code, tests, and docs providing multi-layer validation. The PRD acceptance criteria is supported by verified evidence from implementation, automated tests, and documentation.
